recommended_use: Main cross-sectional estimation file for pay-gap models; strongest public file for geography, commuting, and sample size.
why_it_matters: Huge sample; worker, household, commute, and place-of-work variables; state and PUMA geography; annual earnings and wages/salary income.
formats: CSV, SAS, API, custom IPUMS extracts
geography: Nation, region, division, state, PUMA, limited place-of-work geography
refresh: annual; latest standard files currently include 2024 1-year and 2020-2024 5-year
weights: PWGTP for point estimates; PWGTP1-PWGTP80 for ACS replicate-weight variance
join_keys: YEAR + SERIALNO + SPORDER (or IPUMS YEAR + SERIAL + PERNUM); geography keys: STATE, PUMA, POWPUMA, POWSP
must_have_fields: SEX, AGEP, SCHL, ESR, COW, OCCP/SOCP, INDP, WAGP, PERNP, PINCP, WKHP, WKWN, JWTRNS, JWMNP, JWAP, JWDP, PUMA, POWPUMA, POWSP, PWGTP
cautions: Public files identify sex, not full gender identity. Use ADJINC for dollar variables when needed. Do not compare 2020 ACS 1-year directly to standard ACS years unless you explicitly handle its experimental nature.

recommended_use: Best public source for usual hourly and weekly earnings, usual/actual hours, and overtime/tips/commissions status.
why_it_matters: The outgoing-rotation-group (ORG / earner-study) variables are the cleanest free public U.S. hourly-wage source.
formats: monthly raw files, API metadata, custom IPUMS extracts
geography: Nation, state, limited county/metro indicators in public use
weights: Use EARNWT for earnings/ORG variables in IPUMS CPS; use WTFINL for basic monthly non-ORG analyses
join_keys: YEAR + MONTH + household/person IDs (or IPUMS YEAR + MONTH + SERIAL + PERNUM)
must_have_fields: PESEX/SEX, PEEDUCA/EDUC, PEMARITL/MARST, NCHILD or household child measures, PEHRUSL1/UHRSWORKORG, PEHRACT1/AHRSWORKT, PTERNHLY/HOURWAGE, PTERNWA/EARNWEEK, PEERNUOT or OTPAY, PEERNHRO/PAIDHOUR, PRMJIND1/IND, PRMJOCC1/OCC, PEIO1COW/CLASSWKR, geography fields
cautions: The clean wage sample is wage-and-salary workers in outgoing rotation groups. For annual estimates, stack 12 monthly files carefully and respect ORG weights.

recommended_use: Best single-file deep-control design: longitudinal worker/household analysis with job-spell detail, commuting, pay type, and family context.
why_it_matters: SIPP covers economic well-being, family dynamics, childcare, job spells, hours, pay types, commuting time/miles, union status, and changes over time.
formats: SAS, Stata, pipe-delimited text, documentation PDFs/codebooks
geography: National public-use microdata; limited public geography
unit_of_analysis: person, person-month, and job-spell depending on file design
weights: Monthly/calendar-year cross-sectional weights WPFINWGT; longitudinal weights FINYRn; use matching replicate weights when publishing
join_keys: panel/person identifiers from SIPP files; monthcode/year; job-number suffixes for job modules
must_have_fields: sex, age, education, marital/family variables, childcare/family context, job spell start/end, hours, pay rate, overtime/bonus/tips/commission fields, minutes-to-work (e.g., TJB(n)_PVTIME), commute miles (e.g., TJB(n)_PVMILE), commute mode variables, days worked, time away without pay, union membership
cautions: Variable names often depend on topical module/job number; use the year-specific codebook search and users guide instead of hard-coding every raw field name up front.

recommended_use: Mechanism layer for unpaid care, housework, daily scheduling, work-at-home, and commute-related travel.
why_it_matters: Only federal survey measuring the full range of nonmarket activities with diary timing and location; ideal for work/family/time-constraint channels.
formats: CSV zip bundles, syntax files, custom ATUS-X extracts
geography: National; linked CPS geography where available
weights: Single-year: TUFINLWGT (2006-2019, 2021+), TU20FWGT for 2020; pooled early years use TU06FWGT for 2003-2005; multi-year activity/respondent files may use TUFNWGTP
join_keys: TUCASEID / respondent ID plus line number; link to ATUS-CPS file
must_have_fields: activity codes, duration, start/stop times, activity location, who-with variables, respondent file, ATUS-CPS linked employment/earnings fields, commute/work-travel activity categories
cautions: Time use is person-day rather than person-year. Keep it as a mechanism module, not a naïve merge onto every ACS/CPS observation.

recommended_use: Local labor-market context by geography, industry, sex, education, firm size, and firm age.
why_it_matters: Adds external wage/employment context and firm-composition structure that public person-level files cannot reveal directly.
geography: State, county, workforce investment areas, and more
unit_of_analysis: aggregated cell (geo x time x sex x education x industry x firm characteristics)
join_keys: year/quarter, state/county, industry, sex, education, firm size/age
must_have_fields: EarnS, EmpS, sex, education, industry, firmsize, firmage, geocodes, time
cautions: QWI is contextual, not person-level. Use it as merged external features, not as a replacement for microdata.

why_it_matters: Controls for business-cycle and local labor-market conditions by state/county/metro/city.
formats: BLS time series, downloadable text, API
geography: Nation, state, county, metro, city/town and other local areas
cautions: Choose a stable geographic target (state, county, or CBSA) early so merges stay consistent across datasets.

recommended_use: County/industry employment and quarterly wage context; near-census of covered employment.
why_it_matters: Excellent external controls for industry wage levels, concentration, and local labor demand.
must_have_fields: employment, total wages, average weekly wage, ownership, establishment counts
cautions: Disclosure suppressions exist in narrow cells; build fallbacks to higher aggregation levels.

why_it_matters: Adds local occupational wage norms that complement person-level occupation dummies.
must_have_fields: employment, median wage, mean wage, wage percentiles
cautions: Public geography and occupation definitions vary slightly over time; harmonize SOC vintages.

recommended_use: Occupation task, skill, education, training, and work-context features.
why_it_matters: Gives structured occupation-level controls beyond coarse occupation dummies: time pressure, physical proximity, structured work, required education, etc.
must_have_fields: Job Zones, Education/Training/Experience, Work Context, Skills, Work Activities, crosswalk tables
cautions: You must standardize occupational codes to a single SOC vintage before merging.

recommended_use: Fine-grained neighborhood commuting and job-density context.
why_it_matters: Useful if you want tract/block-level residence-work environment proxies beyond individual commute reports.
must_have_fields: home area characteristics, work area characteristics, origin-destination flows
cautions: Not person-level. Use only if your project truly needs neighborhood commuting context.

recommended_use: Regional price-level adjustment across states and metro areas.
why_it_matters: Nominal earnings gaps are partly shaped by location cost differences; RPP improves cross-place comparability.
must_have_fields: all-items RPP, housing rent RPP (if used)
cautions: This is best for cross-place adjustment, not for within-place inflation over time.

recommended_use: Time inflation adjustment for CPS, SIPP, and ATUS earnings variables.
why_it_matters: ACS provides ADJINC/ADJUST-style factors for its own dollar variables, but other surveys need a common deflator.
must_have_fields: CPI-U all items (or selected deflator choice)
cautions: Use a single explicit base year across all non-ACS sources and document it.

recommended_use: Supplemental expectations and mechanism evidence on reservation wages, wage expectations, and offers; not a drop-in main regression file.
why_it_matters: Gets closer to what workers say they would accept or expect to earn than ACS/CPS/SIPP do, which makes it useful for calibration and supporting evidence on bargaining-related channels.
refresh: every four months; public microdata available with an 18-month lag
weights: Use the supplied survey weights in the released microdata files.
join_keys: Use release identifiers and survey wave/date fields within SCE files; do not attempt person-level merges to ACS/CPS/SIPP
must_have_fields: reservation wage, expected earnings, offered wage, accepted/rejected offer indicators, demographics
cautions: Separate survey, smaller sample, expectation-based, and not designed for person-by-person merges with ACS/CPS/SIPP; best used as supporting evidence or calibration.
